question.text: Scenario: Sarvottam Fintech Ltd. (SFL), an unlisted fintech company, plans to launch its IPO. The company's financials for the last three years show: net tangible assets of Rs.5 crore, Rs.4.2 crore, and Rs.3.8 crore; average operating profit of Rs.17 crore (positive each year); and net worth of Rs.2 crore each year. The IPO is structured as a book building issue with a price band of Rs.280-Rs.320 per share. The total issue size is 50 lakh shares, comprising 35 lakh fresh issue shares and 15 lakh shares as Offer for Sale by the promoter, Harish Mehta. The issue receives the following subscription on closing day (T day): QIB category -- 1.5 crore shares bid (against 25 lakh shares reserved); NII category -- 60 lakh shares bid (against 7.5 lakh shares reserved); RII category -- 52.5 lakh shares bid (against 17.5 lakh shares reserved). The cut-off price is determined at Rs.310 per share. Pooja Sharma, a retail investor, applied for 200 shares at cut-off price through ASBA, with funds blocked in her SCSB account. Vikram Desai, the RTA officer, is coordinating the post-issue process. Question 1 of 5: Based on SFL's financials, is the company eligible for a standard IPO under SEBI ICDR Regulations?

answer.text: No, because the average operating profit is below Rs.20 crore
No, because the net worth of Rs.2 crore each year exceeds the permitted limit
Yes, because all three eligibility criteria -- net tangible assets >= Rs.3 crore each year, average operating profit >= Rs.15 crore, and net worth >= Rs.1 crore each year -- are satisfied
No, because an unlisted fintech company cannot make a public offer under SEBI regulations

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Check each criterion: (1) Net tangible assets: Rs.5 cr, Rs.4.2 cr, Rs.3.8 cr -- all >= Rs.3 crore each year. Pass. (2) Average operating profit: Rs.17 crore >= Rs.15 crore, positive each year. Pass. (3) Net worth: Rs.2 crore >= Rs.1 crore each year. Pass. SFL meets all standard eligibility requirements. **The Trap:** The operating profit threshold is Rs.15 crore, not Rs.20 crore. Candidates who misremember this number will wrongly reject SFL. Also, there is no prohibition on fintech companies making public offers. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series II-A Chapter 7.5 lists eligibility criteria. An additional condition applies if the company changed its name within 1 year (50% revenue from new activity), but that is not mentioned here. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** All three criteria must be met simultaneously. A company strong on profits but weak on net tangible assets still fails. When evaluating an IPO, verify each criterion independently -- don't assume meeting one means meeting all.
